title: Java Software Engineering
source: Authored for the Salareen corporate catalog; structured to mirror the UK apprenticeship standard format.

knowledge.K1: The programmer's problem-solving mindset: decompose, implement, verify and iterate.
knowledge.K2: Java fundamentals: types, variables, expressions and control flow.
knowledge.K3: Object-oriented programming: classes, objects, encapsulation, inheritance and polymorphism.
knowledge.K4: Data structures and Java collections such as lists, sets and maps.
knowledge.K6: Error handling with exceptions and building robust software.
knowledge.K7: Build tools, dependencies and the JVM.
knowledge.K8: Automated testing, unit and integration, for example with JUnit.
knowledge.K10: APIs and HTTP/JSON for integrating systems.
knowledge.K11: Security and reliability practices: input validation, secrets and vulnerabilities.
knowledge.K12: Professional engineering collaboration: version control, code review and conventions.

skills.S1: Decompose a problem into precise, implementable steps.
skills.S2: Write correct Java using types, control flow and methods.
skills.S3: Design classes and objects using OOP principles.
skills.S4: Choose and use appropriate data structures.
skills.S6: Handle errors and edge cases robustly.
skills.S7: Use build tools and manage dependencies.
skills.S8: Write automated tests and refactor with confidence.
skills.S9: Read and write data using SQL databases.
skills.S11: Apply security practices: validate input and manage secrets.
skills.S12: Collaborate using version control and code review.

behaviours.B2: Takes responsibility for code quality and correctness.
behaviours.B4: Acts with integrity on security and quality.

Duty 1.statement: Solve problems and write correct Java code.

Duty 5.statement: Connect software to data and other systems.

Duty 6.statement: Build secure software and collaborate professionally.
